value.found_quote: To prevent `bypassPermissions` or `auto` mode from being used, set `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` or `permissions.disableAutoMode` to `"disable"` in any [settings file](/en/settings#settings-files). These are most useful in [managed settings](#managed-settings) where they cannot be overridden.
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes managed settings as an organization-wide permission policy mechanism. It references a "managed settings" section and explains that administrators can set `permissions.disableBypassPermissionsMode` or `permissions.disableAutoMode` to `"disable"` in managed settings where they "cannot be overridden." This directly substantiates the claim that administrators can enforce organization-wide permission policies (e.g., disabling bypassPermissions or auto mode) via managed settings.
